scopes_skipped.reason: livespec has no live system to probe (no DB / no edge functions); inspection is static-only

inspection.inspection_method: static — dev-tooling/implementation/refresh_gaps.py walked the repo via hardcoded gap-detection predicates

gaps.title: .beads/config.yaml is git-tracked — Open Brain convention is to gitignore it (per-clone host URL drift)
gaps.expected: Per Open Brain's setup-beads.sh comments and OB's top-level .gitignore: .beads/config.yaml is gitignored because the dolt remote URL it embeds is host-specific (HTTPS on Linux/VPS, SSH on macOS), causing per-machine diff thrash if tracked. Livespec's spec defers to OB conventions (canonical pattern source) so the same gitignore entry applies.
gaps.observed: .beads/config.yaml is git-tracked; bd init committed it as part of the first beads-init commit on the bootstrap branch. No `.beads/config.yaml` line in the top-level .gitignore.
evidence: dev-tooling/implementation/setup-beads.sh — re-asserts every required value on each run, so a per-clone config.yaml is fully reproducible
gaps.fix_hint: Add `.beads/config.yaml` to top-level .gitignore and `git rm --cached .beads/config.yaml`. Subsequent `setup-beads` runs re-assert the required values, so the per-clone file regenerates automatically.
